summary: Large game-mode decomp PR for src/melee/gm/gm_1601.c, implementing many former stubs and updating gm_1601.h prototypes and gm/types.h struct fields used by the recovered code. The bot report records 2 new matches, 18 unmatched-item improvements, and 1 small unmatched-item regression in gm_8016A22C.

key_files.role: Main implementation file; replaces multiple stub markers with C bodies, adds helper wrappers/includes, and adjusts existing gm_1601 functions.
Prototype surface updated to match recovered function bodies.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80161154, fn_80165548, fn_801656A8, fn_8016588C, fn_80165AC0, fn_80165FA4, fn_80169000, fn_80169C54, fn_8016A4C8, gm_8016AC44 signatures changed from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or less precise types to concrete signatures

key_files.role: Type recovery support for gm_1601 implementations.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t gains x8, x9, x1C fields; MatchPlayerData gains x38 and x3C fields

what_changed: Implemented/matched a large set of gm_1601.c stub functions listed in the PR body, including match-end ranking/statistics, persistent name-tag statistics, fighter/costume selection helpers, and related game-mode logic.
Added includes used by the new code: placeholder.h, if/ifnametag.h, and pl/plbonus.h.
Changed gm_801603B0 from a scoped #pragma dont_inline block to calls through static inline wrapper chains named gm_801601C4_noinline and gm_80160244_noinline.
Rewrote gm_80160C90 character-name selection into a ternary using wrapper calls to gm_80160980/fn_801609E0 helper chains.
Added a local struct gm_stats in gm_1601.c and used it to express the stat accumulator layout consumed by fn_80161C90.
Replaced at least one raw offset access into gmMainLib_8015EDBC with a typed field/array access: &gmMainLib_8015EDBC()->x18[gm_80164024(arg0)].
Updated gm_1601.h to replace unknown or less precise signatures with concrete return and parameter types for several recovered functions.
Updated gm/types.h to expose fields needed by the recovered code in lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t and MatchPlayerData.
Bot report recorded new 100% matches for gm_80166CCC and fn_801656A8, plus improvements for 18 other gm_1601 items and a -4 byte unmatched-item regression in gm_8016A22C.

smart_moves.text: Kept signature recovery synchronized with the owning header instead of leaving local implementations disconnected from gm_1601.h prototypes.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: corresponding prototype updates in gm_1601.h

smart_moves.text: Recovered struct fields in gm/types.h where the implementation needed named accesses, reducing opaque padding for the touched data layouts.
Used project MIN macro repeatedly for saturating counters in statistics and match totals rather than spelling every clamp as branches.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80161C90 MIN uses for gm_stats counters
raw_diff: gm_8016260C MIN uses for match counters

smart_moves.text: Narrowly removed a pragma dont_inline block around gm_801603B0, though replacement wrapper chains still appear to be a matching tactic requiring careful evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed #pragma push/#pragma dont_inline on/#pragma pop around gm_801603B0
raw_diff: added gm_801601C4_noinline and gm_80160244_noinline wrapper chains

decomp_lessons.lesson: When recovered gm code proves a function signature, update the owning gm_1601.h declaration in the same change so call sites and prototype checks see the concrete type.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8016588C changed from UNK_RET fn_8016588C(UNK_PARAMS) to s32 fn_8016588C(lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t*, s32)
raw_diff: gm_8016AC44 changed from UNK_RET to bool

decomp_lessons.lesson: Struct-field recovery can be justified by recovered implementation use: this PR replaced padding with x8/x9/x1C and x38/x3C fields where gm_1601.c reads those offsets.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8016588C reads arg0->x58[arg1].x8, x9, x1C
raw_diff: fn_80161C90 reads MatchPlayerData x38 and x3C

decomp_lessons.lesson: For ranking and stat accumulation logic, ordinary counted loops over player/team standings are plausible and reviewable forms; this PR uses loops for 4-, 5-, and 6-entry standings arrays.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80162170 loops i/j over 4 player_standings entries
raw_diff: fn_80165AC0 loops over 6 player_standings entries
raw_diff: fn_80165FA4 loops over 5 team_standings entries

naming_conventions.convention: Address-style names remained for unknown functions and data, such as fn_80165AC0, gm_8016AC44, lbl_803B75F8, and lbl_8046B488, rather than inventing unsupported semantic names.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: PR lists address-style function names
raw_diff: implementations retain address-style symbol names

naming_conventions.convention: Temporary or local roles use short generic names where semantics are limited: p/q for player records, t for team record, nt for name-tag data, and counter/ptr for accumulator pointers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80165FA4 uses struct MatchTeamData* t
raw_diff: fn_80162170 uses struct NameTagData* nt

naming_conventions.convention: Recovered struct fields with uncertain semantics remain x-offset style or unk fields, such as x38/x3C and gm_stats.unk0/unk4, rather than speculative names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm/types.h adds x38 and x3C
raw_diff: local struct gm_stats uses unk0, unk4, unk8, etc.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Wrapper chains named *_noinline were used to influence calls to small helpers without retaining a broad #pragma dont_inline block.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed pragma block around gm_801603B0
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The loaded evidence shows the tactic and a bot score report, but does not prove each wrapper chain is required for a specific symbol; treat as review evidence, not a general style rule.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Typed field access was preferred over byte-pointer offset math where a gmMainLib return type had an available field.
Saturating arithmetic was often represented with MIN(value, max), with manual branches retained in some places where the diff shows that form.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80161C90 and gm_8016260C use MIN for 0xFFFF/0xFFFFFFFFU saturation
raw_diff: fn_80162170 uses explicit if (v > 0xFFFF) v = 0xFFFF

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: The PR accepted partial-score improvements as well as exact matches; only gm_80166CCC and fn_801656A8 are reported as new 100% matches by the bot.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment: 2 new matches gm_80166CCC and fn_801656A8
text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment: 18 improvements in unmatched items

review_feedback.text: Matched code increased to 68.85% (+0.04%, +1624 bytes). New 100% matches: gm_80166CCC and fn_801656A8.

The bot also reported 18 improvements in unmatched gm_1601 items, including fn_8016A4C8, fn_80161C90, fn_80164B48, fn_80169C54, fn_80165AC0, fn_80165FA4, fn_80169000, fn_80162170, fn_8016588C, gm_8016260C, gm_80168710, fn_80167638, gm_8016AC44, fn_80169A84, fn_80165548, fn_80160DE8, gm_80160C90, and .sdata2.
The bot reported one unmatched-item regression: gm_8016A22C decreased by 4 bytes, from 75.44% to 74.75%. No human review comments are present in the loaded slice.

follow_up_queries: PR 2529 gm_1601 noinline wrapper chains gm_80160980_noinline3 fn_801609E0_noinline3
gm_1601 fn_80164B48 repeated blocks base 0x2C0 0x2D0 unlock mask
gm_1601 MatchPlayerData x38 x3C field recovery
gm_1601 lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t x8 x9 x1C field recovery

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR metadata, changed file counts, merge state
raw_diff: truncated but includes large portions of gm_1601.c plus full visible gm_1601.h/types.h hunks
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report with match/improvement/regression summary
notes: The raw diff is truncated in the loaded slice, so later gm_1601.c hunks may be incomplete.
No human review comments or reviews are present in the loaded PR slice; reviewer intent cannot be inferred.
The bot report supports two exact new matches and many score improvements, but it does not validate every implementation as fully matched.
Wrapper-chain noinline tactics are visible, but the loaded evidence does not prove which exact wrappers were necessary for which functions.
The PR was merged according to metadata, but merge does not by itself convert every tactic into a global standard.

accepted_candidate_records.title: gm_1601 signature recovery should update gm_1601.h
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2529, recovered gm_1601.c bodies were accompanied by gm_1601.h prototype updates replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or less precise types with concrete signatures for functions such as fn_8016588C, fn_80165AC0, fn_80165FA4, fn_80169000, fn_80169C54, fn_8016A4C8, and gm_8016AC44.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recovered gm_1601 struct field use can justify replacing padding with x-offset fields
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2529 added fields to gm/types.h, including lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t.x8/x9/x1C and MatchPlayerData.x38/x3C, where recovered gm_1601.c code accesses those fields.

source_update_candidates.title: Treat noinline wrapper chains as an explicit matching tactic, not normal cleanup
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2529 removed a #pragma dont_inline block around gm_801603B0 but added chains of small wrappers named *_inner and *_noinline, including triple noinline wrappers for gm_80160980 and fn_801609E0. This appears to be a call-shape matching tactic. If curator finds repeated evidence in other PRs, standards could explicitly say wrapper chains need the same narrow objdiff/regression evidence as pragmas, dummy locals, and other steering tactics.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR gives a concrete example of replacing a pragma with wrapper steering, but evidence is not strong enough to accept as a general standard without curation.

source_update_candidates.title: gm_1601.c owns match-end stat and fighter/costume helper decomp work in this slice
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2529's gm_1601.c changes cover match-end standings/stat accumulation, persistent name-tag stats, and fighter/costume selection helpers. Candidate path fact only; verify against current source organization before adding.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful for search/path triage, but path facts should be checked against current source before promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all functions listed in the PR body fully matched; the bot report names only gm_80166CCC and fn_801656A8 as new 100% matches.
Do not promote the noinline wrapper pattern as accepted style from this PR alone; it is a visible tactic with incomplete per-symbol justification in the loaded evidence.
Do not infer human reviewer approval details; the slice contains no human review comments.
Do not convert address-style names or x/unk fields into semantic names based only on this PR.
